sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, support, infrastructure, audits/oversight metrics, and continued development while Eternl introduces paid Pro plans.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development, Backend infrastructure and operations, User support, Day-to-day administration and operations, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and oversight metrics, Eternl Core rewrite, Eternl Hub, enhanced hardware wallet support, enhanced wallet data export, and governance tooling improvements
Deadline/expiry: 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal asks for ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover around $420,000 of annual operating cost.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested budget is allocated as ₳1,292,500 frontend, ₳587,500 backend, ₳117,500 support, ₳305,500 admin, and ₳47,000 audits.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl has an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs combined.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): If roughly 5,500 users, or about 4.2% of the install base, buy a Pro plan, Pro-plan income would fully cover the annual $420,000 cost.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer says up to $420,000 worth of Ada will be converted into stablecoins and deposited into a public company wallet, with any Ada above that amount returned to the treasury.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Independent evidence for: Eternl has an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs combined.

Independent evidence for: The proposer says up to $420,000 worth of Ada will be converted into stablecoins and deposited into a public company wallet, with any Ada above that amount returned to the treasury.
Independent evidence for: Eternl claims that 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal asks for ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover around $420,000 of annual operating cost." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
